_comment: Nested, location-aware rules. Two kinds: 'base' = the HumanityOS base set (distilled from the Humanity Accord, docs/accord/), 'real' = plain-language summaries of real-world laws with a source to verify. The point is a SMALL, memorable base set plus condensed real laws, not millions of statutes. Data-driven (infinite-of-X): add jurisdictions + rules here, the app picks them up. See docs/design/laws.md.
disclaimer: Plain-language summaries for learning, not legal advice. Real laws change and vary by place; always verify with the cited source before relying on one. The HumanityOS base set is our own framework, not the law of any government.

rules.title: You control your own choices
rules.summary: You have agency and self-determination: control over your own actions, the right to refuse coercion or manipulation, and meaningful choice without hidden constraints.
rules.source: Humanity Accord: Rights and Responsibilities, Fundamental Right 1

rules.title: Your private life is yours
rules.summary: You have privacy of personal communication, confidentiality of your beliefs, votes, and associations, and protection from surveillance without your consent.
rules.source: Humanity Accord: Rights and Responsibilities, Fundamental Right 2

rules.title: You deserve the truth about systems you rely on
rules.summary: You have the right to accurate claims about what a system can do, clear disclosure of its limits and risks, and protection from deceptive assurances. Claiming security or privacy that does not exist is itself a harm.
rules.source: Humanity Accord: Rights and Responsibilities, Fundamental Right 3

rules.title: Your voice in decisions is protected
rules.summary: You can take part in collective decisions safely, with protection against vote tampering or suppression and assurance that your participation is counted accurately.
rules.source: Humanity Accord: Rights and Responsibilities, Fundamental Right 4

rules.title: One person, one voice
rules.summary: Everyone has equal standing: no amplification, suppression, or substitution of voices, and no privilege based on wealth, power, or access.
rules.source: Humanity Accord: Rights and Responsibilities, Fundamental Right 5

rules.title: You can always leave
rules.summary: You have the right to disengage, to revoke consent, and to withdraw without penalty. Participation must stay voluntary.
rules.source: Humanity Accord: Rights and Responsibilities, Fundamental Right 6

rules.title: Some acts are never allowed
rules.summary: Certain acts are prohibited without exception, regardless of intent, belief, culture, authority, or claimed benefit, because they destroy the conditions for human dignity and safety. These include non-consensual sexual acts, and they cannot be consented to or justified by any outcome.

rules.title: Government cannot punish most speech
rules.summary: The First Amendment stops the GOVERNMENT from punishing you for most speech, religion, press, assembly, and petition. It does not force a private person or company (including an online platform) to host your speech, and it has limits (for example true threats and incitement).

rules.title: If accused, you have rights
rules.summary: You have the right to remain silent, the right to a lawyer, and the right to fair process before the government can take your life, liberty, or property. You do not have to answer questions without a lawyer.
rules.source: U.S. Constitution, Fifth and Sixth Amendments

rules.title: You usually need everyone's consent to record a private conversation
rules.summary: Washington is an all-party-consent state: it is generally illegal to record a private conversation or phone call unless everyone in it consents. This is stricter than many other states. (Public settings with no expectation of privacy differ.)

rules.title: Renters have a right to a livable home
rules.summary: A landlord must keep a rental reasonably safe and livable (working heat, plumbing, locks, structural soundness). Tenants have defined rights around deposits, repairs, and notice before eviction.

rules.title: Quiet hours and noise limits
rules.summary: Local noise rules limit loud sound (amplified music, construction, barking) especially at night. Specific quiet hours and decibel limits are set in the county or city code; check yours before assuming.
rules.source: Kitsap County Code, noise ordinance (verify current chapter)
